Manifesto Strategy Archives Principles

Movemental Voices

Leaders helping shape this moment.

Movemental is a growing network of movement leaders committed to movement DNA, scenius formation, credibility amplification, innovative business, and wise AI leadership

Early voices

A growing circle of leaders.

Those who have contributed to the shaping of Movemental through dialogue, critique, and shared exploration.

Alan Hirsch

Author & Missiologist

A foundational voice in missional theology, challenging the structural paradigms of the established church.

Brad Brisco

Missional Strategist

Focused on equipping leaders for local context engagement and the decentralization of ministry models.

JR Woodward

Theologian & Catalyst

Advocating for polycentric leadership structures and a robust theology of culture.

Liz Rios

Movement Architect

Pioneering equitable spaces and voices in church planting and urban movemental frameworks.

Rowland Smith

Network Director

Guiding practical implementation of micro-church networks in complex cultural landscapes.

Lucas Pulley

Practitioner

Integrating deep discipleship with innovative organizational models for sustainable ministry.

Tim Catchim

ApeSt Specialist

Specializing in assessing and equipping vocational diversity within leadership teams.

Rob Wegner

Author & Planter

A leading voice in the Starfish movement, advocating for viral, decentralized church planting.

"The future of the church is not formed in isolation, but in the crucible of shared wisdom."

Why voices matter

AI credibility cannot be carried by one person alone.

The integration of technology and theology requires a multiplicity of voices to prevent blind spots and hubris.

group

Trust is relational

We build tools for the church, and the church is fundamentally relational. Trust in our systems is built through the relationships we maintain with proven leaders.

share

Wisdom is distributed

The complexities of AI require insights from theology, sociology, and practical ministry. No single discipline holds the entire truth.

waterfall_chart

Movements are carried by people

Technology is merely an accelerant. True movemental dynamics are carried by the faithful obedience of practitioners on the ground.

The shared question

How do we lead through AI without losing what makes us human?

This is the central inquiry driving the Movemental dialogue. We are examining the structural implications of AI on missiology.

  • remove Theological implications of generative language in pastoral care.
  • remove Maintaining genuine incarnational presence in a digital-first context.
  • remove The democratization of theological resources vs. institutional gatekeeping.
  • remove Designing digital systems that foster dependence on the Holy Spirit.
  • remove Data ethics and the sanctity of congregational information.
  • remove Accelerating multiplication structures without sacrificing formational depth.

"We are not merely adopting new tools; we are actively shaping the architectural framework of how the church will multiply in the next era."

— A Movemental Contributor

Every robust missional idea is pressure-tested.

Join the conversation. We are actively seeking leaders who want to engage critically with the future of church architecture.
